pi_items.verified_value: Fiji built-in T1 Head sample (256x256 pixels, 129 slices, 16-bit TIFF)
pi_items.source: Downloaded and analyzed T1 Head sample from https://imagej.net/ij/images/t1-head.zip
Verified by analyzing T1 Head TIFF image structure
Matches task.json description and export_result.sh script
Code analysis of verifier.py (line 106 uses hardcoded range 20-1500, not 50-1500)

Image analysis suggests slice 17 has fewest dark pixels (likely narrowest), which falls within [13, 17]. However, cannot independently verify this is the true ground truth of the anatomical narrowest point.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Fiji built-in T1 Head sample, a 256x256 pixel, 129-slice 16-bit TIFF MRI image. Slices 10-19 are valid targets for spinal canal segmentation. The verifier uses a physiological range of 20-1500 for cross-sectional area measurements, but the task metadata incorrectly claims the minimum is 50 instead of 20. The expected output files (CSV measurements, diagnosis text, and segmentation screenshot) match the task requirements.
